name: delegation_winback_plan
notes: Sales-Recovery delegation: a seeded lapsed cohort + a win-back plan ask should DELEGATE to the Sales-Recovery specialist and surface an in-chat plan summary + approval prompt (VT-594). Setup: `setup --onboarded --seed-lapsed-customers 8 --name convo-harness-<id>` then `script <tenant> delegation_winback_plan.json`. Step 1 accepts EITHER honest outcome: (a) a real plan summary + approval prompt (cohort non-empty — the primary expectation given the seed), OR (b) an honest insufficient-data reply if the cohort is empty for this run (with the CL-2026-07-10 deterministic 45d lapsed window + seeded consent, the cohort is non-empty, so (a) is the expected outcome — no percentile non-determinism anymore). Both are asserted here only for 'not a D1 non-answer' and 'not the raw status_query count line' — the ORCHESTRATOR SESSION reading the full json-report/judge transcript makes the final quality call on which outcome occurred and whether it's acceptable. Step 2 confirms the pure customer-count ask still fast-paths to the raw number regardless of what step 1 produced.

steps.message: make me a plan to win back my lapsed customers this month
steps.note: Expect delegation to Sales-Recovery: an in-chat plan summary + approval prompt, OR an honest empty/insufficient-cohort reply — never a D1 non-answer, never silence. Manual review (json-report transcript) verdicts which outcome actually happened. [2026-07-04: assert loosened plan->drafted — the live summary says 'drafted a campaign', behavior correct, assert was too literal.] [VT-633: interim ack allowed; draft asserted against the settled transcript + tenant-wide DB truth.]

steps.message: how many customers do I have?
steps.note: Pure count ask still fast-paths via status_query regardless of the win-back plan outcome above.
